title: Eshkol JIT/AOT/VM parity ledger
description: Records, for each Eshkol operation and builtin, where its AOT, JIT, and VM implementations live and whether they are at parity.

generator_commit.description: git rev-parse HEAD at generation time, for cross-checking when source has moved.

special_forms.description: Forms recognised by the parser as ESHKOL_*_OP — let, letrec, lambda, define, match, etc.

builtins.description: Function-call-shape builtins — gradient, vector-set!, kb-query, etc. Anything dispatched by name in the ordinary call path, not as a special form.

code_location.description: A single point or range in the source tree, recorded as repo-relative paths so the ledger survives clones.

function.description: Containing function or method name, when known.

parity_status.description: See README.md for semantics. Generator may insert 'unverified' but never demote a hand-set status.

aot.description: AOT/JIT codegen entry points — the same code path serves both modes for special forms.

oneOf.description: Special form is compiled away in the VM (e.g. desugared to other ops); no native ID.

items.description: Repo-relative test file path or glob.

aot.description: AOT codegen entry. Empty array means "AOT routes through the generic call path with no specialised codegen" (= calls a runtime C function only).

runtime.description: C runtime functions invoked from AOT (eshkol_*_tagged etc.) — useful for tracing where a divergence actually lives.

oneOf.description: Builtin is AOT-only (e.g. some parallel primitives in non-WASM builds).

vm_entry.description: VM-side mapping. The compiler emits OP_NATIVE_CALL with a numeric native_id; vm_native.c (or one of vm_*.c) handles that ID.

native_id.description: The numeric ID emitted by vm_compiler.c.  Null when the form is recognised structurally without an OP_NATIVE_CALL emit.

compiler.description: Where vm_compiler.c (or vm_parser.c) emits the OP_NATIVE_CALL for this name.

handler.description: Where the native ID is actually serviced (vm_native.c case, or a vm_*.c sub-handler).

wasm_guarded.description: True if the handler body is wrapped in #ifndef ESHKOL_VM_WASM (skipped in browser/WASM builds).
